subject: Social Science - History
chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

key_points: Before British rule, India had an indigenous education system including pathshalas, gurukuls, and madrassas
Orientalists supported teaching Indian languages and classical knowledge
Anglicists supported English education and Western knowledge
Macaulay’s Minute (1835) promoted English as the medium of instruction
Wood’s Despatch (1854) laid the foundation for the modern education system in India
examples: Orientalist view: Teaching Sanskrit and Persian literature
Anglicist view: Teaching Western science and literature through English
Universities established in 1857: Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras
Reformer example: Raja Rammohan Roy supported modern scientific education
important_terms: Indigenous Education: Traditional education system in India before British rule
Orientalists: Scholars who supported Indian languages and classical learning
Anglicists: People who supported English education and Western knowledge
Macaulay’s Minute: A policy supporting English education in India
Wood’s Despatch: Educational reform document of 1854
exam_tips: Remember the difference between Orientalists and Anglicists
Learn the importance of Macaulay’s Minute (1835)
Remember Wood’s Despatch (1854) as the foundation of modern education
Remember the first universities established in 1857
Understand how Western education influenced Indian nationalism
